title: Code quality score below threshold (0.667)
evidence: scores.json: code_quality=0.6666666666666666 — ruff or equivalent linter flagged ~33% of checked items
suggestion: Run ruff check --fix to address lint warnings; .ruff_cache directory exists suggesting ruff was used during scoring

title: Low maintainability score (0.288)
suggestion: Consider breaking soccer_data.py (555 lines) and queries.py (516 lines) into smaller modules; add type annotations to public functions

title: MCP tool error wrapper only catches TeamNotFoundError
evidence: server.py:34 — _safe() catches TeamNotFoundError but not KeyError, ValueError, or CSV parsing errors
suggestion: Wrap with a broader exception handler in _safe() so corrupt data or unexpected inputs return an error payload rather than crashing the tool call

title: Tests use sys.path hack instead of proper package structure
suggestion: Add a pyproject.toml with package config so pytest discovers the package without sys.path manipulation
